faqs.question: Why are fly problems common in Red Hook?
faqs.answer: Red Hook's waterfront location creates humidity and moisture that attracts flies, while the neighborhood's many restaurants, food vendors, and proximity to the Gowanus Canal provide breeding grounds. Older buildings and warehouse conversions may also have structural gaps that allow fly entry.
